would_shift_distribution: Production of the CA00 policy form and endorsement schedule — would clarify whether any exclusions apply and shift mass away from ROR/denial toward clean coverage if none are identified.
Vehicle schedule and driver record confirming the involved vehicle and operator were covered under the policy — would increase clean coverage probability.
Incident/accident report and claimant contact establishing cooperation-clause compliance by the insured — would reduce ROR probability.
Premium payment confirmation and absence of any cancellation/non-renewal notice — would increase confidence in the in-force determination.
Pre-suit demand letter or complaint once filed — would crystallize the claim's value relative to limits and may surface additional coverage questions.
Prior claims history or aggregate exhaustion ledger — would confirm whether the $1,000,000 per-occurrence aggregate remains available.

coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

To: Adjuster of Record
From: Argos Coverage Specialist
Re: REQ-016 / CLM-016 — Auto BI, Serious Severity, Litigated
Policy: PLACEHOLDER-001 (POL-PLACEHOLDER-2026) | CA00 Form | FL
Reviewed As Of: 2026-04-14

─────────────────────────────────────────────
I. THRESHOLD COVERAGE INDICATORS
─────────────────────────────────────────────

1. POLICY IN FORCE
The policy period runs 2026-01-01 through 2026-12-31 and carries status in_force. The loss date of 2026-04-13 falls within that period. (Probability: 0.99)

2. COVERAGE TYPE ALIGNMENT
The targeted coverage, CP-AUTO-BI-STANDARD, is defined as coverage_type: auto_BI. The loss facts describe an auto bodily injury claim. The types are aligned. (Probability: 0.99)

3. APPLICABLE LIMITS
Per-person: $300,000 | Per-occurrence: $1,000,000 | Aggregate: $1,000,000 | Deductible: $0 | SIR: None. No endorsements, schedules, or modifications are on file to alter these figures. (Probability: 0.99)

─────────────────────────────────────────────
II. CLAIM PROFILE AND RISK FACTORS
─────────────────────────────────────────────

The intake summary characterizes this as a 'serious' severity auto BI claim. The litigation flag is True; the claimant is represented by counsel; no complaint has yet been filed. Given the serious severity designation, a pre-suit demand approaching or reaching the $300,000 per-person limit is plausible. The absence of a complaint at this stage may indicate pre-suit negotiations are anticipated.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
III. MATERIAL FILE GAPS (CRITICAL)
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Zero documents were produced with this coverage request. The following items are absent and prevent a complete coverage determination:

• CA00 policy form text and endorsement schedule — exclusions, conditions, covered auto definitions, and driver restrictions cannot be evaluated.
• Declarations page — vehicle schedule and named insured confirmation unavailable.
• Incident/accident report — facts of loss, involved vehicle, driver identity, and fault unknown from primary source documents.
• Cooperation and notice compliance documentation — whether the insured has complied with the CA00 cooperation and timely-notice conditions is unknown.
• Premium payment / cancellation records — no ledger entry confirms continued in-force status beyond structured metadata.
• Prior claims / aggregate exhaustion ledger — aggregate availability unverified.

These gaps are material. No coverage determination that purports to be final should issue until the file is supplemented.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IV. OUTCOME DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Clean Coverage:         0.50
Reservation of Rights:  0.43
Denial:                 0.07
                       ─────
Total:                  1.00

The ROR probability is elevated primarily by the complete absence of policy form documents and the litigated, serious-severity profile — not by any identified exclusion or condition breach. The denial probability is low but non-trivial solely because CA00 exclusions cannot be screened without policy text.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
V. WHAT WOULD SHIFT THE DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────

• CA00 form + endorsement schedule → if no exclusions apply, shifts mass to clean coverage.
• Vehicle/driver records confirming covered auto and permissive operator → increases clean coverage probability.
• Cooperation/notice compliance confirmation → reduces ROR probability.
• Premium/cancellation records confirming in-force status → increases confidence.
• Pre-suit demand or complaint → crystallizes value relative to limits; may surface new coverage questions.
• Aggregate exhaustion ledger → confirms limit availability.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VI. LIMITS NOTICE
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Given the 'serious' severity designation and represented-claimant status, the adjuster should assess whether the $300,000 per-person limit is adequate to satisfy a potential demand. Excess/umbrella notification and bad-faith prophylaxis protocols should be evaluated in accordance with Florida law and the client program guidelines.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTE ON DOCUMENT AVAILABILITY
─────────────────────────────────────────────

All conclusions in this memorandum are drawn exclusively from structured policy metadata and the intake summary. No policy form, endorsement, incident report, or other document was available for review. This analysis is preliminary. A final coverage determination requires document production.

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW ONLY — NOT FOR ISSUANCE WITHOUT ADJUSTER AUTHORIZATION]

[Date]

Named Insured: [Named Insured Name — PTY-NAMED-INSURED-PLACEHOLDER]
[Named Insured Address]

Re:   Policy No.:    PLACEHOLDER-001
      Claim No.:     CLM-016
      Date of Loss:  April 13, 2026
      Claimant:      PTY-CLM-016
      Coverage:      Commercial Auto Bodily Injury (CA00 Form)

Dear [Named Insured],

[Carrier / TPA Name] has received notice of the above-referenced bodily injury claim. We write to acknowledge receipt, to confirm that we are investigating this matter, and to advise you of a reservation of rights pending completion of our coverage investigation.

I. ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF CLAIM

We have received notice of an auto bodily injury claim arising from an incident on April 13, 2026, involving claimant PTY-CLM-016. The matter has been identified as serious in severity. The claimant is represented by counsel, and litigation has been flagged. No complaint has been filed as of the date of this letter.

II. POLICY INFORMATION

The above-referenced policy (PLACEHOLDER-001) is a Commercial Auto policy written on the CA00 form, with an effective period of January 1, 2026 through December 31, 2026. The applicable Bodily Injury liability coverage (CP-AUTO-BI-STANDARD) carries a per-person limit of $300,000 and a per-occurrence limit of $1,000,000, with no deductible or self-insured retention.

III. BASIS FOR RESERVATION OF RIGHTS

While we have commenced our investigation and have confirmed that the loss date falls within the policy period and that the claimed coverage type aligns with the policy's auto BI coverage, our investigation is not yet complete. Specifically, the following items remain unresolved and may bear on the availability of coverage:

1. Covered Auto / Vehicle Schedule: We have not yet confirmed whether the vehicle involved in the incident was a covered auto under the policy's vehicle schedule.

2. Operator Status: We have not yet confirmed whether the driver at the time of the incident was a permissive operator or otherwise covered under the policy's terms.

3. Policy Conditions — Notice and Cooperation: The CA00 policy form imposes conditions including timely notice of loss and a duty to cooperate in the investigation and defense of claims. We are reviewing compliance with these conditions.

4. Applicable Exclusions: The CA00 form contains a number of exclusions that may be relevant to the circumstances of this loss. We are reviewing the facts of the incident against those exclusions and will advise if any appear to apply.

5. Additional Policy Conditions: Other policy conditions may bear on coverage depending on the facts developed during investigation.

IV. RESERVATION

By undertaking investigation and, to the extent applicable, defense of this matter, [Carrier / TPA Name] does not waive and expressly reserves all rights, defenses, and privileges available under the policy, at law, or in equity, including but not limited to the right to disclaim or limit coverage based on any ground identified during the investigation. This reservation of rights is not an admission or denial of coverage.

V. YOUR DUTIES UNDER THE POLICY

We remind you of your obligations under the policy, including the duty to cooperate fully in the investigation and defense of this claim, to promptly provide all requested information and documentation, and to refrain from making any voluntary payments or admissions without our prior written consent.

VI. INDEPENDENT COUNSEL

Because we are reserving our rights, you may have the right under Florida law to retain independent counsel at our expense in connection with the defense of this matter. Please consult with your own legal counsel regarding this right.

If you have any questions regarding this letter or our investigation, please contact [Adjuster Name] at [Contact Information].

Sincerely,

[Adjuster Name]
[Title]
[Carrier / TPA Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

─────────────────────────────────────────────
DRAFT NOTICE: This letter is a preliminary draft generated from structured policy metadata and an intake summary only. No policy form text, endorsements, or incident documents were reviewed. The adjuster must verify all factual recitals, confirm applicable CA00 form language, and review this draft with coverage counsel before issuance. Florida-specific statutory and common-law requirements for reservation of rights letters must be independently verified.

denial_letter.body:
COVERAGE DENIAL LETTER
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW ONLY — NOT FOR ISSUANCE WITHOUT ADJUSTER AUTHORIZATION]
[LOW PROBABILITY OUTCOME — 0.07 — ISSUED FOR COMPLETENESS ONLY]

[Date]

Named Insured: [Named Insured Name — PTY-NAMED-INSURED-PLACEHOLDER]
[Named Insured Address]

Re:   Policy No.:    PLACEHOLDER-001
      Claim No.:     CLM-016
      Date of Loss:  April 13, 2026
      Claimant:      PTY-CLM-016
      Coverage:      Commercial Auto Bodily Injury (CA00 Form)

Dear [Named Insured],

[Carrier / TPA Name] has completed its coverage investigation of the above-referenced matter. Based on that investigation, we are writing to advise that coverage is not available for this claim for the reasons set forth below.

[ADJUSTER INSTRUCTION: This denial letter is a placeholder draft only. At the time of this analysis, NO specific exclusion, condition breach, or coverage bar has been identified in the file. This draft must not be issued unless and until investigation reveals a specific, articulable, document-supported ground for denial — such as a determination that the vehicle was not a covered auto, the operator was not a permissive user, a named exclusion applies, or a policy condition (e.g., cooperation, notice) was materially breached. The specific denial ground(s) must be inserted here, with precise citation to the applicable policy language, before this letter is issued.]

BASIS FOR DENIAL: [TO BE COMPLETED BY ADJUSTER WITH SPECIFIC POLICY LANGUAGE AND FACTUAL BASIS FOLLOWING INVESTIGATION]

Example grounds that would require completion:
• The vehicle involved in the loss was not listed on the covered auto schedule as required by [Policy Form Section ___].
• The operator of the vehicle at the time of the loss did not have permission to use the vehicle, and coverage for non-permissive operators is excluded under [Policy Form Section ___].
• The insured failed to comply with the cooperation clause in [Policy Form Section ___] by [describe specific failure], which has resulted in material prejudice to the carrier's ability to investigate and defend this claim.
• The loss arose out of [describe excluded circumstance] which is excluded under [Policy Form Section ___].

RESERVATION OF ALL RIGHTS

By issuing this denial letter, [Carrier / TPA Name] does not waive any rights, defenses, or privileges available under the policy, at law, or in equity. All such rights are expressly reserved.

You have the right to consult with an attorney regarding this denial. You may also have the right to pursue applicable remedies under Florida law, including but not limited to the Florida insurance code.

If you believe this denial is in error, or if you have additional information that may bear on our coverage determination, please contact [Adjuster Name] at [Contact Information] within [__] days.

Sincerely,

[Adjuster Name]
[Title]
[Carrier / TPA Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

─────────────────────────────────────────────
DRAFT NOTICE: This letter is a low-probability-outcome draft generated from structured policy metadata and an intake summary only. No policy form text, endorsements, or incident documents were reviewed. This letter MUST NOT be issued without (1) identification of specific denial grounds supported by policy language, (2) review by coverage counsel, and (3) adjuster authorization. Florida statutory requirements for denial letters must be independently verified.
